mnemonics.title: ALU Operation Selection
mnemonics.content: SELECTING ALU OPERATIONS: Think of select lines as a DESTINATION ADDRESS that tells the MUX which input to ROUTE to the output - the more select lines, the more destinations you can address.

mnemonics.title: Status Flags - ZONCS
mnemonics.content: REMEMBER FLAGS AS ZONCS: Z = Zero (result is 0), O = Overflow (signed), N = Negative/Sign (MSB=1), C = Carry (unsigned), S = (sometimes) Supplemental. These form ZONCS or ZOCS.

mnemonics.title: Arithmetic vs Logical Operations
mnemonics.content: ARITHMETIC uses NUMBERS (add, sub, mul, div), LOGICAL uses TRUTH (and, or, not, xor). Arithmetic deals with quantity, Logical deals with condition.

mnemonics.title: Two's Complement Subtraction
mnemonics.content: SUBTRACTION = ADD THE COMPLEMENT: To subtract B from A, invert B (NOT B) and ADD 1, then ADD to A. The carry out validates the result.

acronyms.usage: The computational core of CPU performing arithmetic and logical operations
Special register storing operand before operation and result after operation
Selects between different ALU functional units based on operation code
Bits set by ALU indicating result characteristics (Zero, Carry, Sign, Overflow)
Register storing status flags after ALU operations for conditional branching

visualTips.visualization: Picture ALU as a FACTORY with input conveyor belts (operands), control panel (select lines), different production lines (adder, logic unit, shifter), and output conveyor (result). The MUX is the foreman directing which line's product goes out.
Visualize DOMINOES falling - each full adder's carry out triggers the next adder, causing sequential propagation. More bits = more dominos = more delay.
Think of a ODOMETER wrapping around - when you go below zero, you wrap to maximum value. Subtraction is just adding distance in reverse direction on this circular number line.
Think of a SCOREBOARD after a game - Zero flag lights up if score is 0, Carry flag if you exceeded maximum without wrap, Overflow if you crossed into negative score unexpectedly.

analogies.analogy: The ALU is like a SWISS ARMY KNIFE - it has multiple tools (addition blade, AND tool, OR tool, shifter tool) and the handle's mechanism (select lines) chooses which tool extends for the job.
These are like the GAS PEDAL and GEAR SELECTOR in a car - they tell the engine (ALU) HOW HARD to work and WHICH FUNCTION to perform.
The carry flag is like the ODOMETER TRIP in a car - when you go past 9999 (max 4-digit), it wraps to 0000 and a trip meter shows you overflowed. The carry flag does similar for binary addition.

Overflow flag is like a CHECK ENGINE light for signed arithmetic - it warns when the mathematical result cannot be properly represented in the available space, similar to how the light warns of engine problems.
